channel.title: 简报 Jae Daily · 2026年07月11日
channel.description: 国际政治 · 宏观财经 · 前沿科技 · AI 辅助分析

item.title: [未译] US wants Iran to pledge to stop shooting at ships in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: Vice-President JD Vance is among US officials expected to take part in negotiations resuming on Saturday in Oman.

item.title: [未译] SK Hynix Rises After Record US Offering | The Close 7/10/2026
item.description: Bloomberg Television brings you the latest news and analysis leading up to the final minutes and seconds before and after the closing bell on Wall Street. Today's guests are Winnie Cisar, CreditSights

item.title: [未译] Xi Says China’s Backing for Kim and North Korea Won’t Change
item.description: President Xi Jinping said China’s commitment to its friendship with North Korea, support for Kim Jong Un’s leadership and determination to safeguard the two countries’ common interests “will not chang

item.title: [未译] Apple sues OpenAI alleging theft of top-secret information
item.description: Lawsuit marks collapse of relationship between two of the biggest names in Silicon Valley

item.title: [未译] Construction workers, electricians, couriers: How ICE agents use disguises to deceive and detain migrants
item.description: Various reports describe undercover operations in which federal agents allegedly wore utility workers’ clothing and other disguises to detain undocumented people, although ICE denies they were working

item.title: [未译] India, NZ Plan Strategic Partnership Including Maritime Security
item.description: India and New Zealand will elevate their relationship to a “strategic partnership,” as both seek closer trade and security ties.

item.title: [未译] Hong Kong eases proposed health check rules for elderly commercial drivers
item.description: Hong Kong authorities have relaxed a recent proposal requiring mandatory annual medical check-ups for commercial vehicle drivers aged 65 to 69, reducing the frequency to twice in five years following

item.title: [未译] Meta pulls new AI image feature after days of backlash
item.description: Meta's release this week of an AI feature that let people alter Instagram content drew swift blowback.

item.title: [未译] The Hydration-Break World Cup
item.description: “How much can fans conveniently forget in order to enjoy the games? The answer, evidently, is a lot.”

item.title: [未译] Man nearly sucked out of window mid-air on Ryanair plane, passengers say
item.description: The man's wife held onto his legs for around five minutes to stop him from being sucked out, officials say.

item.title: [未译] The Download: Claude’s inner workings and OpenAI’s “super app”
item.description: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. Anthropic found a hidden space where Claude puzzle

item.title: [未译] Spain battles to contain one of its deadliest wildfires as at least 12 killed
item.description: At least four Britons are believed to be among the victims of the blaze, which left another 23 people missing.

item.title: [未译] Police investigating death of Ann Widdecombe as murder
item.description: The staunch Brexiteer was found at her home Thursday having sustained serious injuries, according to police.

item.title: [未译] The Future of Russian Sanctions After the Hormuz Crisis
item.description: US–Iran peace negotiations have stumbled, but if normalcy returns to the Strait of Hormuz, the G7 must be ready to align again on pressuring Russia.

item.title: [未译] As Typhoon Bavi nears, Chinese bloggers warned amateur AI forecasts may be illegal
item.description: As residents of eastern China brace for Typhoon Bavi to make landfall, bloggers have turned to AI weather models to post forecasts on social media, prompting criticism from state media, which warned t

item.title: [未译] Patriot missile license a 'long-term' investment for Ukraine – but can it afford the wait?
item.description: US President Donald Trump’s surprise announcement that he will approve a license for Ukraine to produce its own Patriot missile interceptors signals a potential breakthrough for Kyiv, though experts c

item.title: [未译] Chip giant SK Hynix raises $26.5bn as shares surge in bumper US listing
item.description: The shares surged as much as 17% in their Nasdaq trading debut on Friday following the record-breaking foreign listing.

item.title: [未译] China recovered its first reusable rocket and showed a new way to do it
item.description: "Clearly, they admire the work that's being done by SpaceX and are trying to replicate it."

item.title: [未译] The Definition of Death
item.description: [Revised entry by Adam Omelianchuk and David DeGrazia on July 10, 2026. Changes to: Main text, Bibliography, notes.html] The definition of death involves matters of philosophy and public policy. The p

item.title: [未译] Spain shrugs off Trump’s claim it caved on defense spending
item.description: Spanish officials expressed confusion after the U.S. president said Madrid had "honored" his military expenditure demands.

item.title: [未译] Will Trump Accounts deliver for American children?
item.description: The White House has hailed the project as giving new generations a stake in the American dream, but the scheme has its critics.

item.title: [未译] VW CEO Under Pressure as Labor Unions Torpedo Turnaround Plan
item.description: Volkswagen AG Chief Executive Officer Oliver Blume faces an uphill battle to push through a fundamental overhaul of the carmaker after failing to win initial backing from the supervisory board.

item.title: [未译] "Puedes mandar a toda la UME y a toda la OTAN que no lo vas a apagar": qué hace tan extremo un incendio como el de Almería
item.description: Expertos explican cómo el calor, el viento, la vegetación, los barrancos y las viviendas convirtieron el fuego de Los Gallardos en un incendio extremo Leer

item.title: [未译] Sadiq Khan lobbies for Ed Miliband to become chancellor
item.description: Energy secretary wins support from London mayor but faces opposition from some Labour MPs over his net zero policies

item.title: [未译] Prepare for a perilous summer in markets
item.description: Potential hotspots include a reformist new Fed chair, weak Japanese yen and high-stakes earnings season

item.title: [未译] What’s really going on with mental health?
item.description: Changing terminology risks misleading policymakers and harming those in most need

item.title: [未译] Europe’s slow electrification is a ‘major mistake’, warns IEA chief
item.description: Fatih Birol says EU should have moved faster to achieve energy independence after 2022 gas crisis

item.title: [未译] Rewiring the nation, Burnham-style
item.description: Collaboration is the secret sauce that will make the proposed new way of governing the UK work

item.title: [未译] Tropical nights come to Europe
item.description: The biggest rise in extreme temperatures is not in the daytime

item.title: [未译] Kazakh Tycoon Turlov’s Freedom Raises $300 Million for Expansion
item.description: Kazakhstan billionaire Timur Turlov’s Freedom Holding Corp. raised $300 million in a share sale to help fund its ongoing international expansion.
